steps.title: Find the constant ratio
steps.content: Since the ratio of $k$ to $p$ is constant, we have $$\frac{k}{p} = \text{constant}.$$ Using the given values $k=4$ and $p=3$, $$\frac{k}{p} = \frac{4}{3}.$$

steps.title: Compute k for p=24
steps.content: For $p=24$, multiply the constant ratio by $p$: $$k = \frac{4}{3} \times 24 = 4 \times 8 = 32.$$ The answer is 32.
